_units: median wall-clock in milliseconds from pytest-benchmark default config (warmup absorbed)
_notes: Post-T30 Step 3 baseline. The cumsum FFI in `crates/polars-metal-mlx-sys` now passes input/output as `rust::Slice` (thin pointer+length) instead of per-element `cxx::CxxVector` push/iter loops. With 10M-row inputs the Rust-side push of 10M u8s plus the iter-copy of 10M u32s was costing ~40ms per call on its own; eliminating those loops drops filter_simple from 117.2ms to 77.8ms (-34%) and brings the Metal/CPU ratio for filter_simple under 9x for the first time. The internal MLX `array(ptr, shape, dtype)` constructor still copies bytes into MLX-managed memory and we memcpy the scan result back into the caller's slice — those two memcpys are the only data-touching passes left in the cumsum call. Pushing the FFI cost lower than this requires the M2 MLX-over-MTLBuffer rewrite tracked in docs/open-questions.md.

tpch_q1_modified._notes: Boolean l_returnflag/l_linestatus (encoder 128-bit budget). Filter routes to CPU per M2 cost model; groupby routes to GPU; sort routes to CPU. M2 perf gate: ratio < 1.0. Measured 2026-05-22; Metal median 309.5ms vs CPU median 338.8ms over 5 rounds.

tpch_q1_modified_32bit._notes: All-GPU comparison variant. Boolean l_returnflag/l_linestatus keys; Int32 quantity/shipdate and Float32 price/discount/tax/disc_price/charge value columns. Aggregations use GPU 32-bit dispatchers (no CPU finalize). Measured 2026-05-22; Metal median 26.4ms vs CPU median 26.8ms over 39/29 rounds. The 32-bit path roughly matches CPU latency — both run in ~27ms — because the 32-bit bottleneck shifts from CPU-finalize aggregation to the filter (CPU) and sort (CPU) phases. Without the filter+sort overhead, raw groupby-only timing would show larger GPU advantage.

tpch_q1_modified_32bit_high_card._notes: Q1-shaped query grouped by l_part_class (~1024 distinct groups). Tests the GPU aggregation pipeline under low per-group atomic contention (vs the 4-group degenerate case in tpch_q1_modified_32bit). Hypothesis: GPU should win more substantially here. Measured 2026-05-22; Metal median 65.9ms vs CPU median 66.5ms over 15/13 rounds. Hypothesis NOT strongly confirmed: ratio 0.991 is nearly identical to the 4-group ratio of 0.988. The dominant cost at 10M rows is the filter (CPU-routed, ~9ms on its own) and the sort (CPU-routed, growing with n_groups). The groupby stage itself is a small fraction of total latency at both cardinalities; contention on 4 atomic slots is not the bottleneck at this scale — data movement and CPU-routed stages dominate.
